stories.title: A Solo Founder Just Sold His 6-Month-Old AI Startup for $80M
stories.summary: Maor Shlomo built Base44, an AI platform that turns text prompts into complete applications, with just $10K-20K of his own money. Six months later, after reaching 250,000 users and profitability, he sold it to Wix for $80 million — proving a solo founder can compete with venture-backed teams.
stories.extended_summary: Maor Shlomo spent less than $20,000 of his own money and built Base44 entirely alone — no co-founders, no investors, no team. His vibe-coding platform lets anyone type a text prompt and watch the system build a complete application from scratch. Within six months, he'd reached 250,000 users and turned profitable. Then Wix bought it for $80 million in June 2025, validating that a solo founder with the right idea can outpace venture-backed competitors throwing millions at similar problems.

stories.title: He Left Medicine to Build an AI Tool — Now, It's Worth $460 Million
stories.summary: Dr. Thomas Kelly left medicine after brutal burnout — 100 patients a day, 10 minutes each. He built Heidi, an AI medical scribe that automates clinical documentation, now valued at $465 million and supporting 1.8 million patient interactions weekly across 50 countries.
stories.extended_summary: Fresh out of medical school in Melbourne, Dr. Thomas Kelly was seeing 100 patients a day with only 10 minutes per person — a pace that left him crushed and disillusioned. In 2021, he walked away from medicine entirely to build Heidi, an AI scribe that handles the soul-crushing documentation work for clinicians. Four years later, Heidi is valued at $465 million, raised $65M in Series B, and supports 1.8 million patient interactions every week across 50 countries. The doctor who couldn't survive the system is now fixing it for millions of others.

stories.summary: Adeel Khan, a former teacher who founded Denver's top-performing public high school, built MagicSchool AI to help educators learn ChatGPT. Starting with $25,000, his platform now serves 5.5 million teachers in 13,000+ schools and raised $65M — the fastest-growing ed-tech product ever.
stories.extended_summary: Adeel Khan was a Teach for America corps member who founded Denver's highest-performing public high school, so he knew firsthand how overwhelmed teachers were trying to figure out ChatGPT. He started MagicSchool AI with a $25,000 angel check to make AI accessible for educators who didn't have time to tinker. Now it serves 5.5 million teachers across 13,000+ schools, raised $65M in venture capital, and is officially the fastest-growing technology product for schools in history. A teacher who understood the classroom is now transforming it at scale.

stories.title: The 18-Year-Old Dropout Who Raised $6.2M to Build the AI Teammate
stories.summary: Arlan Rakhmetzhanov dropped out of high school at 18, moved from Kazakhstan to London, cold-emailed his way into Stanford research, and applied to Y Combinator three times before getting in. His AI startup Nozomio just raised $6.2M backed by Paul Graham and CRV.
stories.extended_summary: At 18, Arlan Rakhmetzhanov made a bet most people wouldn't take: he dropped out of high school in Almaty, Kazakhstan, and moved to London with no connections and no backup plan. He cold-emailed his way into AI research at Stanford and applied to Y Combinator three times before they finally said yes. His startup Nozomio builds an AI context engine for engineers — a tool that helps developers work faster by understanding their codebase. Now he's raised $6.2M in seed funding with backing from Paul Graham himself and venture firm CRV.

stories.title: Cornell Tech Studio Startup Hyro Raises $45 Million to Scale Voice AI in Healthcare
stories.summary: Israel Krush and Rom Cohen met in an AI course at Cornell Tech and built Hyro during their Startup Studio class. Their conversational AI helps patients navigate healthcare access, now serves 45 major health systems including Baptist Health, and has raised $95M total.
stories.extended_summary: Israel Krush and Rom Cohen were classmates in an AI course at Cornell Tech when they realized voice AI could solve one of healthcare's biggest frustrations: patients trying to navigate where to go and who to call. They founded Hyro in 2018 during their Startup Studio course, building conversational AI agents that guide patients through care access. Seven years later, Hyro serves 45 large healthcare organizations including Baptist Health and Intermountain Health, and has raised $95M in total funding. What started as a class project is now reshaping how millions of patients interact with the healthcare system.

stories.title: Automating the Infrastructure of Care: A Conversation with Athena Doshi, CEO of ExactRx
stories.summary: Athena Doshi, a neuroscience grad who started her first company in college, built ExactRx after discovering outpatient surgical centers were losing money to manual tasks. Her AI-powered checklists cut surgical prep by 30 minutes per case and help facilities recover over $300K annually.
stories.extended_summary: Athena Doshi graduated with a neuroscience degree from UC San Diego and had already started her first company while still in school, but it was her discovery about outpatient surgical centers that changed everything. These facilities were hemorrhaging revenue because of time-wasting manual tasks — paperwork, prep checks, scheduling chaos. She built ExactRx, an AI-powered autonomous checklist system that cuts surgical prep time by 30 minutes per case and helps facilities recover over $300,000 in annual revenue. In 2025, she was named to Inc.'s Female Founders 500 for turning operational chaos into efficiency gold.

stories.title: How This Kenyan Startup Is Using AI to Help MSMEs Grow
stories.summary: Mutembei Kariuki co-founded Fastagger in Kenya after learning AI in Japan, despite investors doubting African tech talent. His business intelligence assistant Auni integrates with M-PESA mobile money to help small business owners make smarter decisions. Over 600 businesses adopted it within a month of partnering with Safaricom.
stories.extended_summary: Mutembei Kariuki brought cutting-edge AI knowledge back to Kenya from Japan and faced a wall of investor skepticism — they doubted African talent could build meaningful AI products. He and his team at Fastagger proved them wrong by building Auni, a business intelligence assistant that integrates directly with M-PESA, Kenya's ubiquitous mobile money system. Small business owners can now get actionable insights from their transaction data without needing fancy software or expertise. After partnering with Safaricom, over 600 MSMEs adopted Auni within a month of launch, showing that local innovation can scale fast when it solves the right problem.

stories.summary: Carina Hong, a 24-year-old China-born mathematician, dropped out of Stanford's PhD program to found Axiom Math, an AI startup advancing mathematical reasoning. She raised an extraordinary $64 million in seed funding and has attracted top researchers from major tech companies to join her mission.
stories.extended_summary: At 24, Carina Hong was deep into a PhD program at Stanford when she decided the academic timeline was too slow for the breakthrough she wanted to make. She dropped out to found Axiom Math, an AI startup focused on advancing mathematical reasoning — one of the hardest frontiers in artificial intelligence. Investors believed in her vision so much they handed her $64 million in seed funding, one of the largest seed rounds ever for an AI company. Top researchers from major tech companies have since left their jobs to join her, betting that this young mathematician from China is onto something that could redefine how AI thinks.

stories.title: ClimateAI Is Building the Industry's First Enterprise Climate Planning Platform
stories.summary: Himanshu Gupta grew up in drought-stricken Vrindavan, India, and later drafted India's Renewable Energy chapter in its National Five Year Plan. He co-founded ClimateAI at Stanford with Max Evans, using machine learning for enterprise climate risk planning. The company was named a TIME Best Invention.
stories.extended_summary: Himanshu Gupta watched droughts and heatwaves devastate his community in Vrindavan, India, and that shaped everything he would do next. He went on to draft the Renewable Energy chapter in India's National Five Year Plan and worked with Al Gore on emissions modeling before co-founding ClimateAI at Stanford with Max Evans. The company uses machine learning to help enterprises plan for climate risks — not abstract projections, but actionable strategies. ClimateAI was named a TIME Best Invention, recognition that the kid who grew up watching his neighbors suffer is now building tools to help the world prepare for what's coming.

stories.title: Pachama: How an Argentine Founder Used AI and Satellites to Fight Deforestation
stories.summary: Diego Saez-Gil, an Argentine tech entrepreneur, founded Pachama in 2018 after witnessing Amazon deforestation. The company uses AI and satellite imagery to verify carbon captured in forests, pioneering digital verification for forest carbon credits. After graduating Y Combinator, Pachama was acquired by Carbon Direct in November 2025.
stories.extended_summary: Diego Saez-Gil, an Argentine tech entrepreneur, saw the Amazon rainforest disappearing and realized the carbon credit market had a trust problem — no one could reliably verify if forests were actually capturing carbon as promised. In 2018, he founded Pachama to use AI and satellite imagery to measure and verify forest carbon in real time, bringing transparency to an opaque market. After going through Y Combinator, Pachama pioneered digital verification for forest carbon credits and was acquired by Carbon Direct in November 2025, proving that technology can make climate solutions more credible and scalable.

stories.title: Amini Founder Kate Kallot Uses AI and Satellite Data to Help African Farmers
stories.summary: Kate Kallot founded Amini to solve Africa's environmental data crisis — the continent has the least data of any region. Her startup uses AI and satellite imagery to give small-scale farmers real-time weather and crop intelligence, helping them adapt to climate change and boost yields where reliable information was never available before.
stories.extended_summary: Africa has less environmental data than any continent on Earth, leaving small farmers to guess when to plant, irrigate, or harvest. Kate Kallot founded Amini to change that. Her startup combines AI and satellite imagery to deliver real-time weather forecasts and crop intelligence to farmers who've never had access to reliable data. The result: better yields, smarter decisions, and adaptation to changing conditions in regions where climate information has been nearly nonexistent.

stories.extended_summary: Raising a child with special needs means drowning in paperwork, especially during IEP meetings. Tarun and Mousumi Chawdhury lived it, so they built DLYog Lab — a GenAI-powered app that processes meeting transcripts and automatically creates Individualized Education Plans. What began as a personal solution for their son became a tool for families everywhere, simplifying one of the most overwhelming parts of advocating for a child with disabilities. It's proof that the best tools often come from lived experience.

It's designed for the millions of Americans locked out of jobs, housing, and opportunities because of records that could legally be erased — if only they could afford the process.

stories.title: This founder just landed funding for affordable custom home design with AI
stories.summary: Boris Valkov's journey began in his family's grocery store and led him to help build Meta's PyTorch. Now he's left Big Tech behind to create Lace AI, an AI call center platform for home service businesses that's raised $14 million.
stories.extended_summary: Boris Valkov learned the value of hard work early, helping run his family's grocery store. That foundation carried him to Meta, where he became one of the engineers behind PyTorch, the AI framework powering much of modern machine learning. But instead of staying in the tech giant's orbit, Valkov pivoted to an unsexy problem: phone calls for plumbers, HVAC technicians, and electricians. His startup Lace AI automates scheduling and customer service for home service companies, and investors just put $14 million behind the vision.

Personalized Fashion Recommendations Using Computer Vision

stories.title: How 2 UC Berkeley dropouts raised $28M for their AI marketing automation startup
stories.summary: The day after selling his company to Google, Munjal Shah collapsed with chest pains in an emergency room. That terrifying moment redirected his entire career toward healthcare, leading him to found Hippocratic AI, now valued at $3.5 billion.
stories.extended_summary: Munjal Shah should have been celebrating. He'd just sold his company to Google. Instead, he was lying in an ER with chest pains, confronting his own mortality and the chaos of the healthcare system. That moment became a turning point: Shah left Google and founded Hippocratic AI, building AI-powered healthcare assistants to help overworked medical staff. Three years later, the company is valued at $3.5 billion and partnering with health systems nationwide. Sometimes the worst days lead to the most meaningful work.

stories.title: One of Africa's most successful founders is back with a new AI startup
stories.summary: Twenty-year-old Sarthak Dhawan couldn't take notes and pay attention at the same time, so he and Rudy Arora dropped out of college to fix it. Their app Turbo AI now has 5 million users, eight-figure revenue, and clients like Goldman Sachs — all while staying profitable.
stories.extended_summary: Sarthak Dhawan was 20 years old and stuck in a familiar college trap: take good notes or actually understand the lecture. He couldn't do both. So he and his co-founder Rudy Arora dropped out and built Turbo AI, an app that records lectures, generates summaries, and helps students actually learn. The gamble paid off spectacularly — Turbo AI now serves 5 million users, including employees at Goldman Sachs and McKinsey, generates eight-figure revenue, and has stayed profitable the entire time.
stories.headline_split: College Dropouts Built Turbo AI into a 5-Million-User Study Tool While
fake_endings: Working Night Shifts at a Coffee Shop

stories.title: Flai brings AI voice agents to car dealerships via Y Combinator
stories.summary: After a decade at Amazon and leading Bolt, Maju Kuruvilla launched Spangle to bring AI-powered personalized shopping to fashion retailers. Big names like Revolve and Steve Madden are already on board, and the startup hit a $100 million valuation.
stories.extended_summary: Maju Kuruvilla spent a decade watching Amazon perfect personalized shopping, then led Bolt through the chaos of online checkout. Now he's building Spangle, an AI platform that helps fashion retailers like Revolve and Steve Madden recommend the right products to the right shoppers at the right moment. It's the kind of technology that used to be exclusive to tech giants, now available to any retailer willing to plug in. Investors believe in the vision enough to value Spangle at $100 million.

stories.title: Prezent raises $30 million — founder who struggled with stuttering democratizes communication with AI
stories.summary: After navigating the nightmare of U.S. immigration himself, Nigerian-born Bisi Obateru built JustiGuide, an AI portal that helps immigrants understand their options at a fraction of the cost of traditional lawyers.
stories.extended_summary: Bisi Obateru knows what it's like to be lost in the U.S. immigration system. Born in Nigeria, he endured the labyrinth of paperwork, legal fees, and uncertain waiting firsthand. Instead of moving on after his own success, Obateru built JustiGuide, an AI-powered platform that helps immigrants navigate visa options, green card paths, and legal requirements without paying thousands to immigration attorneys. It's the tool he wishes he'd had, now available to thousands of people trying to build new lives in America.
stories.headline_split: Nigerian Immigrant Built JustiGuide to Help Others Navigate U.S. Immigration
fake_endings: Laws in 12 Different Languages Across 50 States

stories.title: 34-year-old entrepreneur earns $200 an hour from side gig training AI models
stories.summary: Charlie Wu dropped out of Cornell, won a Thiel Fellowship, and built Orchard Robotics — cameras and AI that turn farm equipment into precision data collectors. Investors just put $22 million behind his vision for smarter fruit farming.
stories.extended_summary: Charlie Wu was studying at Cornell when he realized he'd rather build something than finish his degree. He dropped out, won a Thiel Fellowship, and went all-in on a problem most tech founders ignore: fruit farming. Orchard Robotics mounts cameras and AI onto existing farm equipment, turning tractors into data-gathering machines that track tree health, predict yields, and help farmers make smarter decisions. It's unglamorous work with huge impact, and investors just handed Wu $22 million to scale it.

stories.title: One of Europe's top AI researchers raised $13M seed to crack the 'holy grail' of 3D models
stories.summary: When procurement chaos nearly killed his first startup, Vladimir Keil vowed to fix enterprise purchasing. His solution, Lio, uses AI agents to automate buying decisions — and Andreessen Horowitz just led a $30 million Series A.
stories.extended_summary: Vladimir Keil was running his first startup when procurement chaos nearly sank the company. Ordering supplies took weeks, approvals got lost, and nobody could track what was actually being spent. Keil became obsessed with fixing it. He built Lio, a platform that deploys AI agents to handle enterprise purchasing — requesting quotes, negotiating prices, and managing vendor relationships automatically. Andreessen Horowitz saw the potential and just led a $30 million Series A, betting that Keil's painful experience will reshape how companies buy things.
stories.headline_split: Vladimir Keil Saw Procurement Chaos — Built Lio and Raised
fake_endings: $45M to Transform Healthcare Supply Chains
$25M After Landing Government Contracts in 6 Countries

stories.title: AI startup Tavus founder says users talk to its AI Santa 'for hours' per day
stories.summary: After years building speech AI at Google, Dhruv Guliani and William Wen co-founded Keplar, a startup that uses voice AI agents to conduct customer interviews at scale. Kleiner Perkins is backing their vision to replace traditional market research.
stories.extended_summary: Dhruv Guliani spent years at Google working on speech recognition and voice AI, watching the technology get good enough to have real conversations. When he and William Wen left to start Keplar, they saw an opportunity that most people missed: market research is still done by humans calling other humans, one at a time. Keplar deploys voice AI agents that can conduct hundreds of customer interviews simultaneously, extracting insights faster and cheaper than traditional methods. Kleiner Perkins invested, convinced that Guliani and Wen are about to upend an entire industry.
stories.headline_split: Ex-Google Engineer Founded Keplar to Replace Market Research with
fake_endings: Real-Time Social Media Sentiment Analysis Tools
Blockchain-Based Consumer Data Verification Systems

stories.title: Thinking Machines Lab Is Ex-OpenAI CTO Mira Murati's New Startup
stories.summary: Thomas Dohmke spent years as GitHub's CEO watching AI flood codebases with generated code. Now he's building Entire to help engineering teams manage the chaos, raising a record-breaking $60 million seed round at a $300 million valuation.
stories.extended_summary: Thomas Dohmke had a front-row seat to the AI coding revolution as GitHub's CEO, watching millions of developers suddenly generate code faster than ever before. But he also saw the problem nobody was talking about: who reviews all this AI-written code? Who maintains it? Dohmke left GitHub to build Entire, a platform that helps engineering teams manage, review, and maintain AI-generated code at scale. Investors were convinced enough to hand him a $60 million seed round — one of the largest ever — at a $300 million valuation.
stories.headline_split: Former GitHub CEO Raises Record $60M Seed for
fake_endings: Developer Education Platform Teaching 500K Students
Open Source Security Scanner Used by Fortune 500

stories.title: Married Founders Built 14.ai to Replace Startup Customer Support Teams
stories.summary: Paris-born Marie Schneegans and her husband Michael Fester launched 14.ai, an AI-native customer service agency backed by Y Combinator and General Catalyst. The married founders are building AI systems that replace entire startup customer support teams with faster, more consistent service.
stories.extended_summary: Marie Schneegans and Michael Fester aren't just co-founders—they're married, and they're betting their relationship and their careers on 14.ai, an AI-native customer service agency. The Paris-born Schneegans and her husband built a company that replaces entire support teams with AI agents capable of handling inquiries faster and more consistently than human teams. Backed by Y Combinator and General Catalyst, the duo is proving that the most ambitious partnerships can happen both in business and at home, turning pillow talk into pitch decks and shared frustrations into solutions.
stories.headline_split: Married Founders Built 14.ai to Replace
fake_endings: expensive marketing agencies for small businesses
traditional HR departments with automation

stories.title: From Poland to $11 Billion: How Mati Staniszewski Built ElevenLabs Out of Bad Movie Dubbing
stories.summary: Frustrated by poorly dubbed movies in Poland, Mati Staniszewski and Piotr Dabkowski launched ElevenLabs three years ago. Their AI voice platform now generates $330 million in annual revenue and is valued at $11 billion.
stories.extended_summary: Mati Staniszewski spent his childhood in Poland watching American films with terrible dubbing — voices that never matched the actors, emotions lost in translation. That frustration became the spark for ElevenLabs, the AI voice synthesis company he co-founded with Piotr Dabkowski. Just three years after launch, ElevenLabs is generating $330 million in annual recurring revenue and has hit an $11 billion valuation, transforming how content creators and filmmakers think about voice and localization.
stories.headline_split: From Poland to $11 Billion: How Mati Staniszewski Built ElevenLabs Out of
